title: client: attach failure wedges terminal (alt-screen + cursor hidden); graceful failure UX
description:
Repro: \`phux attach <session>\` when no server is running, or when the session doesn't exist, or when the client gets signal-killed mid-attach. Terminal is left in alt-screen mode + cursor hidden + sometimes raw mode. User has to type \`reset\` blind.

Root causes:
1. \`attach::run\` installs RawModeGuard (alt-screen + raw mode + hide cursor) BEFORE the UDS connect + handshake. Any pre-handshake failure flickers the terminal through alt-screen for nothing.
2. RawModeGuard::Drop only runs on Rust unwinding. SIGTERM/SIGKILL/SIGHUP bypass it. The cleanup escapes (\\x1b[?1049l + \\x1b[?25h) never get written.
3. No signal handler installed to restore terminal state on signal exit.
4. Auto-spawn server always uses DEFAULT_SESSION_NAME ('default') but client asks for whatever was on the CLI. Mismatch causes attach to fail at handshake while client is already in alt-screen.
5. Connection errors surface as 'attach failed: connection refused' — actionable would be 'no phux server at <path>; start one with \`phux server\`' with the exact suggested command.
6. Stale socket files survive a crashed server (separate ticket — file follow-up).

Acceptance for THIS ticket:
- attach::run defers RawModeGuard installation until AFTER handshake + ATTACH succeeds (or skip alt-screen entirely on early failure).
- SIGINT/SIGTERM/SIGHUP handlers installed that run terminal cleanup before exit.
- ConnectionRefused/NotFound socket errors produce a one-line clean error WITHOUT entering alt-screen.
- Auto-spawn passes the user's requested session name through to the spawned server.
- Test under the new \`Screen\` harness: a synthetic 'no server' scenario captures stdout and asserts NO alt-screen-enter byte sequence appears.

Out-of-scope: stale-socket cleanup (file follow-up bd if not already covered).

title: feat(server): wire INPUT_KEY/INPUT_MOUSE/INPUT_FOCUS dispatch in handle_client
description: handle_client in runtime.rs falls through input frames to the catch-all _ arm, silently dropping every keystroke from attached clients. Wire FrameKind::InputKey/InputMouse/InputFocus to the existing PaneActor input mpsc via ServerState. Add a wire-level integration test that drives an InputKey frame through a real UDS connection and verifies it reaches the PaneActor PTY (not just direct PaneActor exercise like pty_pump.rs). Validation: drop with warn! on unknown pane_id or unattached client. Closes the gap noted in phux-byc.6.5 description.
close_reason: Closed

title: byc.8: server-side Terminal placement — spawn_local + LocalSet OR actor pattern
description:
Coming concern for byc.8 (server ATTACH handler). libghostty Terminal is !Send + !Sync (holds NonNull<TerminalImpl>, no unsafe impl Send/Sync in libghostty source).

runtime.rs:258 currently uses tokio::spawn (Send-required) for per-client handler tasks. Today the handler holds no Terminal — only logs unhandled frames. byc.8 will add real ATTACH handling that needs to own a Terminal per pane.

Three viable shapes — byc.8 must pick one:

1. spawn_local + LocalSet — keeps current_thread runtime per ADR-0003; Terminal stays on the runtime thread; preferred.
2. Dedicated pane-supervisor task that owns the Terminal; channels (mpsc) for input/output. The 'actor model' that was flagged earlier in the session as a possible refactor for ServerState shape. Cleaner separation but more plumbing.
3. RefCell<Terminal> behind Rc on a single-threaded executor. Discouraged — fights tokio's spawn conventions.

Do NOT try Arc<Mutex<Terminal>> — won't compile (Mutex requires Send for cross-thread sharing; Terminal isn't Send).

Decision needed at start of byc.8 work. ADR-worthy if the actor pattern is picked.

title: Per-client capability downsampling: define ColorSupport + downsample fn + per-client storage
description:
SPEC §6.2 conformance: `crates/phux-server/src/grid.rs:121-122` unconditionally
emits `Color::Rgb` with a comment that says "the wire carries the final pixel
value" — directly violating SPEC §6.2: "a client advertising Indexed256 MUST
never receive truecolor RGB cells; the server must downsample."

## Design seam: per-pane capture vs per-client fanout

Three options were considered:

1. First-attach floor — capture in the lowest tier any current client
   advertises. Rejected: locks downsampling into the canonical state; a
   later-attaching TrueColor client cannot recover precision.
2. Per-tier cache — capture once in TrueColor, materialize one cached
   diff per active tier. Rejected as premature; complicates invalidation
   and is unnecessary until profiling shows it.
3. On-demand downsample at fanout — capture once in canonical TrueColor;
   each per-client fanout call downsamples to that client's advertised
   tier. Selected. Matches the existing "server owns canonical state,
   clients see derived views" model and keeps the diff-compute path tier-
   agnostic.

## Scope of THIS ticket (substrate only)

- `ColorSupport` enum (TrueColor | Indexed256 | Indexed16) in phux-protocol.
- `downsample(Color, ColorSupport) -> Color` (as helper fn, since `Color` is a
  re-export of libghostty-vt's StyleColor — no inherent impl possible).
- Per-client cap storage on `AttachedClient` in phux-server::state.
  Defaulted to `ColorSupport::TrueColor` (most-permissive; clients without
  advertised caps don't get downgraded).
- New `phux-server::downsample` module with `downsample_op` /
  `downsample_cell` for byc.5's fanout to plug into.
- Comprehensive unit + property tests.
- `diff_spike` MUST remain byte-identical — downsample is per-fanout, not
  per-capture; nothing in the capture path changes.

## Out of scope (follow-up tickets)

- HELLO ColorSupport wiring (SPEC §6.1 conformance — separate task).
- CIELAB perceptual color distance for downsample (better Indexed16 picks).
- Fanout layer itself (byc.5).
close_reason: Substrate landed in cc30ab5. ColorSupport enum + downsample_color + per-client color_support on AttachedClient + phux-server::downsample helper module + comprehensive tests (unit + proptest). diff_spike byte-identical. Follow-ups: phux-7lf (HELLO wiring), phux-i02 (CIELAB), phux-982 (Cow signature).

title: Wire frames: ATTACH/ATTACHED/DETACH/DETACHED + INPUT_KEY/MOUSE/FOCUS/PASTE
description:
phux-byc.4 surfaced that the message-catalog frames from SPEC §7.1-§7.5 + §9 are missing from phux-protocol's FrameKind enum. byc.4 landed the server-side state scaffold but its 5 wire-level integration tests had to be deferred until these variants exist. Until this lands, the byc epic cannot demonstrate end-to-end attach. Likely belongs as a phux-6yl follow-up; filing standalone for visibility.

## Required Skills
rust,tlv,protocol-design
design:
phux-protocol::wire::frame::FrameKind currently has: Hello, Ping, PaneDiff.

Add (per SPEC §7.1-§7.5 + §9):

  C → S
    Attach { session_name: String, role: ?? }   // §7.1
    Detach                                       // §7.3
    InputKey(KeyEvent)                           // §9.1
    InputMouse(MouseEvent)                       // §9.2
    InputFocus(FocusEvent)                       // §9.3
    InputPaste(PasteEvent)                       // §9.4
    InputRaw(Bytes)                              // §9.5 (only if a passthrough is needed; can defer)

  S → C
    Attached { session_id, window_id, pane_id, snapshot: PaneSnapshot }   // §7.2
    Detached                                                              // §7.3
    Bell { pane_id }                                                       // §7.6
    OscEvent { ... }                                                       // §7.7 (defer; not needed for byc.5)
    Alert { ... }                                                          // §7.8 (defer)

Plus the SessionId tagged-union on the wire (per ADR-0007 §3): protocol-level
SessionId is u32 newtype; on-wire it's tagged with the addressed satellite.

For each frame:
  - Add a FrameKind variant
  - Add TLV field IDs in wire/field.rs (per phux-6yl.4)
  - Add encoder + decoder, mirroring how PaneDiff is wired
  - Add an insta hex snapshot covering at least one representative payload
  - Round-trip proptest if the variant has structured payload

phux_protocol::ids::SessionId (wire-side u32) vs phux_core::ids::SessionId
(slotmap key, opaque). They are distinct by design — wire SessionIds are
allocated by the server, slotmap keys are an in-process registry detail.
Add a server-side ::from(phux_core::ids::SessionId) -> wire-side SessionId
mapping + reverse lookup. Don't define a `From` impl in phux-protocol
itself (would force a phux-core dep on phux-protocol).

Verify:
  cargo nextest run -p phux-protocol
  cargo run -p phux-server --example diff_spike   # untouched
acceptance_criteria: FrameKind has all the C→S and S→C variants from SPEC §7. Round-trip + insta snapshots cover each new variant. phux-server's deferred byc.4 integration tests now compile (filing a separate ticket for them).
notes: Discovered by phux-byc.4 agent retro. Blocks byc.5 (e2e test), 9gw.3 (attach loop), and the e2e harness epic phux-a87.
close_reason: Landed in fa335e1. Added FrameKind variants Attach/Detach/InputKey/InputMouse/InputFocus/InputPaste (C->S) and Attached/Detached/Bell (S->C) per SPEC §7.1-§7.5 + §9.1-§9.4. Encoders/decoders mirror PaneDiff pattern, 13 insta snapshots + 7 proptest round-trips. Non-obvious decisions for byc.6: (1) AttachRole {Primary=0, Viewer=1} is phux-defined (not in SPEC §13) -- minimum useful split. (2) PaneSnapshot {cols, rows, ops: Vec<DiffOp>} is minimum per SPEC §8.4 -- cursor/modes/scrollback are TODO. (3) Detached carries no body this wave (SPEC §7.3 {reason, message} deferred). (4) FrameKind dropped Eq (now PartialEq only) because MouseEvent has f64. (5) Added DecodeError::UnknownEnumValue {field, value} for libghostty atom round-trip safety. NOT touched: phux-core, phux-server (per task scope), no From<phux_core::ids::SessionId>. Deferred: OscEvent §7.7, Alert §7.8, InputRaw §9.5. All quality gates green: fmt, clippy -D warnings, nextest (67 protocol + 204 workspace), diff_spike, cargo deny.

title: From impls for all four input event types + server-side KeyEncoder pipeline
description: In phux-server: implement field-for-field conversion from phux_protocol::input::{KeyEvent, MouseEvent, FocusEvent, PasteEvent} to the corresponding libghostty_vt::{key, mouse, focus, paste}::Event types. Wire the key encoder pipeline: per-pane KeyEncoder, set_options_from_terminal(&term) before each encode, encode_to_vec(&event, &mut buf). The encoder lives server-side; the wire never carries encoder options (ADR-0006). Mouse/Focus/Paste have their own libghostty encoders that follow the same pattern.
design:
New module: crates/phux-server/src/input/
  key.rs    — impl From<&phux_protocol::input::KeyEvent> for libghostty_vt::key::Event + PerPaneKeyEncoder
  mouse.rs  — impl From for MouseEvent + per-pane mouse encoder
  focus.rs  — impl From for FocusEvent + DEC 1004 gating
  paste.rs  — impl From for PasteEvent + bracketed-paste decision
  mod.rs    — re-exports

PerPaneKeyEncoder holds a libghostty_vt::key::KeyEncoder + reusable Vec<u8>. Calling sites: `encoder.set_options_from_terminal(&term); encoder.encode_to_vec(&event, &mut buf)`. Mouse/focus follow the same shape.

Encoder lives server-side. Never serialized to wire. See ADR-0006 rationale.

Conversion is infallible (libghostty event types are supersets / equal-sets of ours).
acceptance_criteria: Unit test: round-trip a KeyEvent through the From impl and assert all fields preserved. Conversion is infallible (no Result).
notes:
Spec refs:
  SPEC.md:783-818 — §9.1.6 Server-side key encoding pipeline
  SPEC.md:871-878 — §9.2.2 Server-side mouse encoding pipeline

title: Server pane plumbing: PTY + per-pane diff loop
description:
Wire a real pane: spawn child PTY, feed bytes into libghostty_vt::Terminal, run grid::capture each frame, compute DiffOps, gate by FRAME_ACK/snapshot fallback per SPEC §6. Pools RenderState/RowIterator/CellIterator per pane (the spike allocates each call). Single-pane, no IPC yet — that's phux-byc.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `cargo run -p phux-server --example one_pane bash` spawns a real shell, accepts typed input from stdin, prints DiffOp summaries to stderr per frame.
- [ ] Steady-state capture loop allocates zero bytes (validated by criterion bench or dhat-rs heap trace).
- [ ] Clean shutdown on child exit (no zombie process, no panic on EOF).
- [ ] diff_spike example continues to print "replay invariant: snap0 + diff(0→1) == snap1   ✓" after the refactor.
- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p phux-server` green; clippy -D warnings clean.

title: test-harness: Screen helper (libghostty oracle for client render bytes)

title: server: PTY EOF (shell 'exit') doesn't notify client — client freezes in alt-screen
description:
User repro: typed 'exit' in the pane's shell. Shell exits. Client TUI froze; no path back to the outer shell except Ctrl-b d (detach) or kill.

Current behavior: pane_actor.rs:598 handles PtyEvent::Eof by detaching the PTY-read branch (self.pty_rx = None) and reaping the child, then keeping the actor alive 'for snapshot/input drain'. The server never broadcasts a state change. runtime.rs only emits FrameKind::Detached when the *client* requests detach (line 590).

Desired behavior options:
(a) On last-pane-EOF in the attached session, server sends FrameKind::Detached so the client unwinds its alt-screen guard cleanly.
(b) Server sends a new PaneClosed frame; client picks another pane to focus, or sends Detach if none remain.
(c) Server keeps the empty pane in place but allows the user to respawn via a re-seed command.

(a) is the minimum viable behavior — matches tmux/screen 'session closed' UX. (b) is the long-term shape once windows/panes have richer lifecycle ops; tracks SPEC §10. Pick one as the immediate fix and file (b) follow-on if (a) ships first.

Also: confirm the PaneActor's 'keep alive for snapshot/input drain' comment still makes sense once we notify clients on EOF — a closed pane shouldn't accept new input, and snapshots of a dead pane have unclear semantics.
close_reason: Fixed in commit ad93cd7 on fix/phux-it8-eof-detach: PaneActor now fires an internal exit_notify oneshot on PTY EOF; the runtime's per-pane EOF watcher broadcasts FrameKind::Detached to attached clients and detaches them. Test: crates/phux-server/tests/eof_detach.rs (pty_eof_drives_detached_frame_to_attached_client). Multi-pane (phux-9gw) deferred via TODO.

title: client/server: app inside pane doesn't receive expected keys (e.g. htop 'q' to quit)
description:
User repro: ran phux attach demo, ran htop inside, pressed 'q' to quit — no effect. Likely root cause: phux advertises TERM=ghostty to the inner shell (pane_actor.rs:271). Ghostty's terminfo declares kitty keyboard protocol support, so apps that detect this can flip into CSI-u mode where every keypress (including 'q') becomes a CSI-u escape like \x1b[113;1u. Apps that don't natively understand kitty protocol (htop, less, many old TUIs) won't recognize the quit key.

Investigation needed:
- Trace the exact bytes phux's per-pane key encoder writes when 'q' is pressed inside htop (instrument crates/phux-server/src/input/key.rs around the Event::encode call).
- Check whether htop's terminal-init turns on kitty modes (CSI > 1 u). If so, the encoder will produce CSI-u bytes; if not, plain ASCII.
- Decide policy: (a) advertise TERM=xterm-256color until ghostty's kitty support is mature in phux's path; (b) make ghostty terminfo correct for our path; (c) downgrade kitty protocol when the inner app hasn't requested it (which should already be the case — confirm).

Related: phux-19e (smarter client stdin parsing) is about the opposite direction. This ticket is about server-side encoding mismatching the inner app's expectations.
close_reason: 3120f40 — fix(server): default TERM=xterm-256color to unbreak htop quit

title: SnapshotSynthesizer: distinguish wide-cell tails from blank cells via cell.wide()
description:
Surfaced in 460faeb. crates/phux-server/src/grid.rs::synthesize emits a space for any cell where cell.graphemes().is_empty() — covers both genuinely-blank cells AND wide-cell tails (the right half of '東', emoji, etc.). Affects snapshot fidelity for wide-character content.

Fix: use libghostty's CellIteration::raw_cell() then Cell::wide() to discriminate. When wide() indicates a tail position, skip emission (the base grapheme already advanced the cursor past it). Otherwise emit space.

Test: round-trip a snapshot containing CJK + emoji through Synthesizer → fresh Terminal → assert grid equivalence. Use the existing synthesizer_round_trips_via_libghostty test as the pattern.

Blocks: nothing critical, but affects byc.8's PANE_SNAPSHOT correctness for any pane content with wide graphemes.

title: phux-server::downsample: handle image protocols + keyboard protocol gating + OSC 8 stripping
description:
Follow-up to ADR-0013 byte-stream rewriter landing. The v0 rewrite_bytes() in phux-server::downsample handles truecolor SGR -> indexed256/16 only. SPEC §6.2 requires additional gating per client caps:

- Image protocols: drop or transform sixel (DCS) / kitty graphics (APC G) / iTerm2 (OSC 1337) when the client did not advertise the matching ImageProtocol bit. The OSC/DCS/APC passthrough is permissive today; needs a capability-gated drop path.
- Kitty keyboard protocol: APC keyboard-reply sequences must be stripped from the outbound stream for clients that did not negotiate the matching kbd_protocols bit. The canonical Terminal still processes them locally.
- OSC 8 hyperlinks: strip when ClientCapabilities.hyperlinks == false.
- ITU-style colon separators: the rewriter currently only recognises ';'. tmux handles both 'CSI 38;2;R;G;B m' and 'CSI 38:2::R:G:B m'; add the colon variant for parity.

Scope: code only (crates/phux-server/src/downsample.rs + tests). No SPEC changes needed — §6.2 already mandates the behavior; this is implementation catch-up.

title: SPEC cleanup: §12 flow control + §2 terminology + Appendix B + §11.1 ADR ref + §6.2 RenderingMode
description:
Post-ADR-0013 SPEC inconsistencies surfaced by the §8 rewrite agent (commit 2071f8f). Out-of-scope of that commit; needs a single small cleanup pass:

1. §12 Flow control still references PANE_DIFF and frame_id-based FRAME_ACK. Update to PANE_OUTPUT and per-pane seq.
2. §2 Terminology 'Frame' definition still uses frame_id. Reword to: 'frame: a server-emitted PANE_OUTPUT carrying a contiguous batch of VT bytes for one pane, identified by a monotonically increasing per-pane seq.'
3. Appendix B Reserved ranges still mentions DiffOp tag allocation guidance. Drop or replace with NamedKey/ErrorCode-only guidance.
4. §11.1 'What is deliberately absent' cross-references ADR-0002 (superseded). Repoint to ADR-0013 or to the appropriate post-supersession ADR.
5. §6.2 RenderingMode { Diff | VtReplay } kept for forward-compat but deprecated in prose; a future major-version SPEC removes the field entirely. File a separate v0.2 ticket if/when major bump happens.

Single-commit cleanup. Scope: SPEC.md only. No code touched.

title: client: smarter stdin VT byte parsing (escape sequences, CSI, SS3)
description: phux-9gw.3 ships a minimal stdin byte parser that recognises ASCII chars, a few control chars, and the detach chord — but does not parse arrow keys, function keys, modifier-prefixed sequences, mouse reports, focus reports, or bracketed paste. Lift this into a proper VT input parser (likely sharing logic with libghostty if a parser surface lands upstream, or hand-rolled) so the attach loop forwards the full key/mouse/focus event spectrum upstream.

title: client: implement VIEWPORT_RESIZE wire frame + client SIGWINCH wiring
description: The phux-9gw.3 attach loop installs a SIGWINCH handler but currently has nowhere to send the new viewport — SPEC §10.5 defines VIEWPORT_RESIZE but it is not yet a FrameKind variant. Add the variant in phux-protocol, then wire the client to encode and send it on SIGWINCH.

title: phux-protocol: add ERROR FrameKind variant per SPEC §14

- An ErrorCode enum (or just newtype u16) matching SPEC §14's code table (SESSION_NOT_FOUND=102, plus the others enumerated in SPEC §14).
- FrameKind::Error { code: ErrorCode, message: String } (SPEC §14 carries a human-readable message).

title: phux-server: handle ATTACH frame and respond with ATTACHED + PANE_SNAPSHOT
description:
The server's per-client task in crates/phux-server/src/runtime.rs::handle_client today only handles FrameKind::Ping. The match arm for FrameKind::Attach / FrameKind::Detach / FrameKind::Input* falls through to a debug log and the connection sits idle. byc.6 (the integration tests) is blocked on this handler existing.

Under ADR-0013 the response sequence to ATTACH is bytes-on-wire:

1. Resolve target -> SessionId via ServerState.
2. Allocate per-client mpsc + spawn writer task draining into the UnixStream.
3. Build SessionSnapshot from the registry; send FrameKind::Attached { snapshot, initial_client_id }.
4. For each pane in the focused window: call phux_server::grid::SnapshotSynthesizer::synthesize(&terminal) to produce { cols, rows, vt_replay_bytes }, then send FrameKind::PaneSnapshot { pane_id, cols, rows, vt_replay_bytes, scrollback_bytes: None } per SPEC §8.4 / §13.
5. Subsequent live output flows as FrameKind::PaneOutput { pane_id, seq, bytes } -- the per-pane PTY pump + downsample::rewrite_bytes path that byc.5 covers.

On FrameKind::Detach: send Detached and drop the writer.

On AttachError::UnknownSession: send an ERROR frame with code SESSION_NOT_FOUND (102 per SPEC §14). Requires the ERROR variant — see follow-up phux-byc.9.

Acceptance: byc.6 tests #1, #2, #5 must pass once this and the ERROR-variant ticket land.

## Required Skills
rust,tokio,ipc,libghostty
design:
On receiving FrameKind::Attach { target, viewport, request_scrollback, scrollback_limit_lines }:
1. Resolve target → SessionId via ServerState (AttachTarget::ByName lookup is implemented; ById / Last / CreateIfMissing per SPEC §13).
2. Allocate a per-client mpsc channel and call ServerState::attach(client_id, name, tx).
3. Spawn a writer task draining the mpsc into the UnixStream's write half.
4. Build a SessionSnapshot from the registry and send FrameKind::Attached { snapshot, initial_client_id }.
5. For each pane in the session's active window, send FrameKind::PaneSnapshot per SPEC §13 attach sequence.

On FrameKind::Detach: send FrameKind::Detached, then drop the writer (which closes the socket on the server side).

On AttachError::UnknownSession: send an ERROR frame with code SESSION_NOT_FOUND (102 per SPEC §14). Requires the ERROR variant — see follow-up ticket.

Acceptance: byc.6 tests #1, #2, #5 must pass once this and the ERROR-variant ticket land.

title: Conform DiffOp to SPEC §8.1/§8.5: cursor + modes as PANE_DIFF fields, not ops
description:
SPEC §8.1 defines `PANE_DIFF` as carrying `cursor: CursorState`, `modes: PaneModes`, and `base_frame_id`/`revision` as **struct fields**, not as entries in the `ops` list. SPEC §8.5 is explicit on the rationale: "pulling them out into separate messages would increase wire chatter for no benefit." The current implementation models cursor as `DiffOp::CursorMove { row, col }` (op-byte 0x03) and `DiffOp::CursorStyle { visible, shape, blink }` (op-byte 0x04), and the `PaneDiff` frame body has no cursor / modes / base_frame_id / revision fields.

Why now: scope is small (one server example, one client mirror, a handful of tests). As more compute/apply/render code lands, the op-stream shape will calcify and the migration will be harder. Wire format is breaking — no external consumer yet — so do it before anyone latches.

Surgical edit list:
- crates/phux-protocol/src/diff/op.rs: delete `DiffOp::CursorMove` and `DiffOp::CursorStyle` variants
- crates/phux-protocol/src/diff/cursor.rs (new): re-home `CursorState`, `CursorShape`, add `PaneModes` bitset
- crates/phux-protocol/src/wire/diff.rs: delete op-byte 0x03/0x04 encode/decode + proptest strategy entries
- crates/phux-protocol/src/wire/frame.rs: `PaneDiff` gains `base_frame_id: u64`, `cursor: CursorState`, `modes: PaneModes`, `revision: u8`
- crates/phux-protocol/src/wire/decode.rs: decode the new fields
- crates/phux-protocol/src/wire/field.rs: add field-ID constants for new PaneDiff fields
- crates/phux-protocol/src/diff/compute.rs: stop emitting cursor ops; return cursor + modes alongside ops
- crates/phux-client/src/mirror/apply.rs: drop CursorMove/CursorStyle match arms; consume cursor+modes from PaneDiff
- crates/phux-client/src/mirror/state.rs: track modes (or pipe through)
- crates/phux-server/examples/diff_spike.rs and one_pane.rs: update to new compute_diff signature
- Tests: regenerate snapshots, append new cursor-field + modes round-trip tests, replace existing CursorMove/CursorStyle tests
close_reason: Landed in fd89d93dc712c4d46aee8f5066ffaa846f59e7d8. CursorState + PaneModes + base_frame_id + revision fields added to PaneDiff per SPEC §8.5; op-stream cursor ops deleted. New diff_spike md5: b84a5890eda2277734faad33b7411c74. Follow-up phux-bxa filed for MouseProtocol/MouseEncoding enums.

title: Wire ColorSupport through HELLO/ClientCapabilities per SPEC §6.1/§6.2

title: Add constructors + non_exhaustive to public structs in phux-protocol
description:
Follow-up to the enum non_exhaustive pass. Six public wire types (SessionInfo, WindowInfo, PaneInfo, SessionSnapshot, ViewportInfo, PaneSnapshotPayload) currently allow struct-literal construction from external crates. For a published crate, adding any field is a breaking change.

## Approach
1. Add a `new(...)` constructor or builder for each type taking the load-bearing fields, with sensible defaults for the rest.
2. Add `#[non_exhaustive]` to each struct.
3. Refactor crates/phux-protocol/tests/{frame_wire_snapshots,wire_roundtrip}.rs to use the constructors (currently 20+ struct-literal sites).
4. Verify cargo nextest + clippy stay green.

## Why
Pairs with the enum non_exhaustive landed alongside (AttachTarget, LayoutNode, SplitDir). Together they make additive evolution of phux-protocol non-breaking for third-party clients — which is the whole point of the published-crate stance per ADR-0008/0010.

## Cascade
Blocks: nothing currently. Should land before the first crates.io publish (phux-protocol 0.1.0).

title: Migrate wire encoder to TLV (SPEC Appendix A)
description:
Migrate the phux-protocol wire encoder from positional field encoding to TLV per SPEC.md Appendix A: {field_id: varint, wire_type: u8, value}.

## Why
The current encoder in crates/phux-protocol/src/wire/encode.rs writes fields positionally. This makes ANY extension to an existing frame body a wire-breaking change: e.g., adding a field to PaneSnapshotPayload, SessionInfo, or ATTACH requires a major-version bump because decoders cannot skip unknown trailing fields without TLV envelopes.

SPEC.md Appendix A mandates TLV precisely to make additive evolution backwards-compatible:
- Field IDs in crates/phux-protocol/src/wire/field.rs are ALREADY defined but unused by the encoder/decoder — they were laid down anticipating this migration.
- §6 obliges decoders to skip unknown fields by length, which TLV's self-delimiting structure makes mechanical.

## Cascade
This commit's spec-conformance pass for ATTACH/ATTACHED/PANE_SNAPSHOT/SessionInfo/WindowInfo/PaneInfo (followup to phux-4az) papers over the issue: it matches SPEC §13 shapes exactly but keeps the positional encoder. Any future field addition to those shapes is a breaking wire change today.

## Scope
- Replace primitive writes in Encoder/Decoder with field-tagged writes consuming field.rs constants.
- Define varint and tagged-union (wire_type=7) encoding per Appendix A.
- Maintain MAX_FRAME_LEN, big-endian, and existing decode-error semantics.
- Update all FrameKind variants to emit TLV bodies.
- Regenerate all wire snapshots.
- Add round-trip tests for unknown-field skipping (forward-compat invariant).

## Out of scope
- Schema versioning beyond what SPEC §6 already prescribes.
- Removing positional helpers used by sub-records (DiffOp encoding is unchanged unless we choose to TLV-ify it; that's a separate decision).

title: phux-client attach loop: connect, render mirror, propagate input
description:
The base attach loop that 9gw.1 (predictive echo) was implicitly waiting on. Connects to a phux-server over UDS, sends HELLO + ATTACH, accumulates diff frames into a DiffMirror (9gw.2), forwards local keypress/mouse/focus/paste events upstream, and renders the mirror to the outer terminal (crossterm or direct VT writes). This is what makes 'phux attach <session>' a working command.

## Required Skills
rust,tokio,tui,vt-rendering
design:
Module: crates/phux-client/src/attach/
  connection.rs  — UDS connect, framed read/write halves
  loop.rs        — main attach loop (tokio select! over: server frames, stdin bytes from outer terminal, resize signals)
  render.rs      — VT emission from DiffMirror to stdout (crossterm or raw escapes; pick simplest first)
  input.rs       — read raw VT bytes from stdin, parse via libghostty's input layer, encode as INPUT_KEY/INPUT_MOUSE/etc, send upstream

Lifecycle:
  - parse args (server socket path, session name)
  - enter raw mode + alt screen on outer terminal
  - connect UDS, exchange HELLO, send ATTACH
  - on receive ATTACHED { snapshot }: ingest_snapshot into DiffMirror, full render
  - loop:
      tokio::select! {
        frame = framed_reader.next() => apply ops to mirror; redraw changed region
        stdin_bytes = stdin.read() => parse to InputEvent[]; send each upstream
        sigwinch = signal::unix::signal(SIGWINCH).recv() => send RESIZE to server
        ctrl+b d (or whatever the detach key is) => send DETACH; exit cleanly
      }
  - on exit: restore outer terminal (leave alt screen, disable raw mode)

Scope guard: does NOT implement predictive echo (that's 9gw.1, layers on top of this).

Depends on:
  - phux-9gw.2 (DiffMirror) ✓
  - phux-byc.3 (server runtime + UDS listener)
  - phux-byc.4 (ATTACH/DETACH lifecycle)
  - phux-6yl.* (wire codec, input event encoding)

Verify:
  cargo nextest run -p phux-client
  Manual: terminal A runs `phux serve`, terminal B runs `phux attach default`,
    typed keys appear in the inner shell and inner shell output renders.
acceptance_criteria: phux attach <session> connects, renders the active pane, propagates input, and detaches cleanly. cargo nextest run -p phux-client green. Manual test in two terminals works.

Manual two-terminal smoke test deferred until phux-byc.8 (server ATTACH handler) lands — handle_client only handles PING today. Three follow-ups filed: phux-4hp (VIEWPORT_RESIZE wire frame + SIGWINCH wiring), phux-631 (configurable detach key), phux-19e (smarter VT input parser).

description:
Full E2E harness that spawns a phux-server process with a PTY-backed pane, attaches a synthetic phux-client over the wire codec, drives keystrokes, and verifies the round-trip: client input -> server -> PTY child -> Terminal -> diff frame -> client mirror -> rendered Grid. Catches regressions across the whole stack.

## Required Skills
rust,tokio,integration-testing
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A nextest target spins up the server in-process, connects a client harness, runs a scripted scenario (type 'echo hi\n', expect 'hi' in the mirror) and exits clean.
- [ ] A second scenario covers reconnect: client detaches mid-stream, reconnects, sees a fresh snapshot then resumed diff stream.
- [ ] A third covers multi-client: two clients attach the same session, both see the same pane after keystrokes.
- [ ] CI runs the harness on every PR (added as a workflow job in .github/workflows/ci.yml).
- [ ] Wall time under 10s for the full scenario set.

title: phux-client: diff mirror — accumulate incoming Grid diffs into local state
description:
phux-client needs a local Grid mirror that consumes incoming diff frames from the server (CellRun/Clear/CursorMove/CursorStyle per phux-6yl.5) and produces a current-state Grid the renderer reads. Prerequisite for both attach-loop rendering AND predictive echo (9gw.1).

## Required Skills
rust,vt-rendering,libghostty
design:
Spec refs:
  SPEC.md:407-440 §8 hot-path frame model
  SPEC.md:487-535 §8.3 Diff operations
  SPEC.md:536-567 §8.4 Snapshots
  ADR/0002-diff-based-protocol.md

Module: crates/phux-client/src/mirror/
  state.rs    — DiffMirror { grid: libghostty Grid, cursor: Cursor, frame_id: u64 }
  apply.rs    — fn apply(state: &mut DiffMirror, ops: &[DiffOp])
                  per-op: CellRun → write cells at (row, col),
                          Clear → blank N cells from (row, col),
                          CursorMove → update cursor,
                          CursorStyle → update cursor attrs.
  snapshot.rs — fn ingest_snapshot(state: &mut DiffMirror, snap: &Snapshot)
                  full-state reset (used on first ATTACH or recovery).

Replay invariant test (mirrors diff_spike from the server side):
  Given a starting Grid G0, a server-produced diff D such that G0.apply(D) = G1,
  the client's DiffMirror starting at G0 applies D and produces a Grid
  byte-identical to G1.

  Reuse the server's diff_spike example output as a fixture if practical.

Tests in crates/phux-client/src/mirror/tests.rs (or tests/mirror_replay.rs):
  - apply CellRun + read back exact bytes
  - apply Clear over a populated row, assert blanked
  - CursorMove updates cursor; CursorStyle propagates attrs
  - Random-walk proptest: arbitrary sequence of DiffOps applied to an
    empty Grid produces the same Grid as applying them via the server's
    capture+diff path (round-trip with the server-side replay invariant)
acceptance_criteria: DiffMirror::apply is invariant w.r.t. server-side replay (client mirror after applying diff bytes == server Grid that produced them). Proptest passes. cargo nextest run -p phux-client green.

title: Integration test: server + synthetic client message exchange
description:
End-to-end harness that spins up a phux-server process (or in-process runtime), drives it with a synthetic client over the wire codec, and asserts the full HELLO → ATTACH → INPUT → diff-stream → DETACH lifecycle. Captures the byc epic's overall acceptance criteria.

## Required Skills
rust,tokio,integration-testing
design:
New: crates/phux-server/tests/end_to_end.rs

Harness approach (in-process, no subprocess fork):
  let socket = tempdir().path().join('phux.sock');
  let server = tokio::spawn(phux_server::runtime::run_with_shutdown(socket.clone(), shutdown_rx));
  // give it a moment to bind
  let client = UnixStream::connect(&socket).await?;
  let (mut reader, mut writer) = phux_protocol::codec::framed(client);

Scenarios to assert (one #[tokio::test] each, or one parameterized):

1. handshake_roundtrip:
   - Client sends HELLO with proto version
   - Server responds HELLO_ACK with negotiated capabilities (SPEC §6)
2. attach_returns_snapshot:
   - Client sends ATTACH(session='default')
   - Receives ATTACHED with SessionId + a pane snapshot frame
3. input_routes_to_pane:
   - After ATTACH, send INPUT_KEY('a')
   - Assert the pane's input log contains the encoded byte (stub from byc.4)
4. detach_clean_shutdown:
   - Send DETACH; receive DETACHED; the connection drains; no panics.
5. server_survives_mid_frame_disconnect:
   - Client writes a partial frame (half a length prefix) then drops the
     stream. Server must NOT panic; the per-client task exits cleanly.

Then shutdown_tx.send(()).await; server.await??; assert socket file unlinked.

Note: this test depends on byc.3 (runtime+socket) and byc.4 (ATTACH/DETACH).
Should be the LAST byc task to land.
acceptance_criteria: All 5 scenarios pass. cargo nextest run -p phux-server --test end_to_end green. No flakes under --test-threads=1.

title: phux-server: ATTACH/DETACH lifecycle + multi-client keystroke merge
description:
Implement the ATTACH/DETACH message handlers and the multi-client merge semantics: multiple clients attached to the same session see the same pane, with their keystrokes interleaved in arrival order at the PTY.

## Required Skills
rust,tokio,ipc
design:
Spec refs:
  SPEC.md:238-282 §7.1/7.2 Client/Server message catalog (ATTACH, ATTACHED)
  SPEC.md:283-308 §7.3 DETACH/DETACHED
  SPEC.md:955-1015 §10.1-10.2 Sessions/Panes

Builds on byc.3's per-client task and byc.1's Registry.

Server-side state:
  pub struct AttachedClient {
      id: ClientId,
      session: SessionId,
      tx: mpsc::Sender<ServerFrame>,  // server → client outbound frames
  }
  pub struct ServerState {
      registry: phux_core::Registry,
      attached: HashMap<ClientId, AttachedClient>,
      pane_subscribers: HashMap<PaneId, Vec<ClientId>>,
  }

ATTACH handler:
  - On ATTACH(session_name), look up SessionId (or create if not found and
    allowed by attach flags), register the AttachedClient in pane_subscribers
    for the active pane.
  - Send ATTACHED response with SessionId + initial pane snapshot.

DETACH handler:
  - Remove ClientId from pane_subscribers; close mpsc tx; respond DETACHED.

INPUT_KEY / INPUT_MOUSE / INPUT_FOCUS / INPUT_PASTE handlers:
  - For each input message from an attached client, route it to the
    server-side per-pane channel that ultimately writes to the PTY (PTY
    wiring lives in phux-bc1.2; for now route to a stub Pane::ingest_key
    method that just records the event).
  - Order: a SINGLE mpsc per pane consumes inputs from all attached
    clients. Whatever ordering the tokio scheduler delivers IS the
    arrival order — this is the multi-client merge contract.

Test (crates/phux-server/tests/multi_client_merge.rs):
  - Spin up server with one session/window/pane
  - Connect two synthetic UnixStream clients, both ATTACH same session
  - Each client sends a sequence of distinct INPUT_KEY events
  - Assert: the per-pane input log contains every key from both clients
    exactly once, in some interleaving (not strictly deterministic, but
    every key appears).
acceptance_criteria: ATTACH/DETACH round-trips. Two attached clients on the same session both see the same active pane. Per-pane input log records every keystroke from both clients exactly once. Integration test green.
close_reason: wave 6 swarm (byc.4 state scaffold + mdBook + crates.io). Wire integration deferred to phux-byc.6 pending phux-4az.

title: phux-server: tokio current-thread runtime + Unix socket listener
description:
Wire phux-server's main loop on a tokio current-thread runtime. Bind a Unix domain socket at $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/phux/phux.sock (or fallback), accept client connections, hand each off to a per-client task that speaks the wire codec from phux-6yl.4.

## Required Skills
rust,tokio,unix-sockets
design:
Spec refs:
  SPEC.md:96-116 §4 Transport (UDS, framing assumptions)
  SPEC.md:117-149 §5 Framing (length-prefixed TLV from phux-6yl.4/5)

New: crates/phux-server/src/runtime.rs
  pub struct ServerRuntime { registry: phux_core::Registry, socket_path: PathBuf, ... }
  pub fn run(socket_path: PathBuf) -> Result<(), ServerError>:
    - Build tokio Builder::new_current_thread().enable_all().build()
    - block_on(async move { serve_loop(...).await })

serve_loop:
  - Bind UnixListener::bind(socket_path)
  - Loop: accept().await → spawn client task
  - On SIGINT/SIGTERM (use tokio::signal): drain accept loop, drop listener,
    unlink socket file. Existing client tasks get a cancellation token.

Per-client task:
  - Frame reader: phux_protocol::codec::FrameReader<UnixStream read half>
  - Frame writer: phux_protocol::codec::FrameWriter<UnixStream write half>
  - For byc.3 specifically: just LOG every incoming frame and respond with
    PONG to PING. Full message routing comes in byc.4.

Socket path resolution: \$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/phux/phux.sock if set,
else /tmp/phux-\$UID/phux.sock. Create the parent dir with 0700 perms.

Refuse to run if the socket file already exists AND a process holds it
(connect-probe). If stale, unlink and proceed.

Tests:
  - integration test in crates/phux-server/tests/socket_lifecycle.rs:
    spawn server in a temp dir, connect a tokio UnixStream, send a PING,
    read PONG, shut server down via the cancellation token, assert socket
    file is removed.
acceptance_criteria: Server binds UDS, accepts a connection, round-trips PING→PONG. Clean shutdown unlinks socket. Integration test in tests/socket_lifecycle.rs green.
close_reason: wave 5 swarm (4 agent retries + manual completion of all 4)

title: Selection / copy-mode protocol + libghostty selection APIs
description:
tmux copy-mode is one of its defining features; we need a peer. libghostty PR #12794 (merged 2026-05-24) adds selection APIs to libghostty-vt's C surface: select_word, select_line, select_all, select_output, format_buf, selection_adjust, selection_order, selection_contains, selection_equal. title: Predictive local echo (Mosh-class UX), client-side
description: Implement Mosh-style predictive local echo in phux-client: client maintains a prediction overlay against its diff mirror, dim-renders predicted cells, reconciles on server FRAME_ACK (SPEC §6). Transport-agnostic — Unix socket today, QUIC later. Mandated by ADR-0007.
design:
Module: crates/phux-client/src/predict/
  state.rs    — PredictionState { pending: VecDeque<Prediction>, mirror: Grid }
  overlay.rs  — render predictions over the diff mirror (dim attribute)
  reconcile.rs — on FRAME_ACK, walk pending predictions and drop confirmed ones; on mismatch, snap mirror to authoritative state

Algorithm (Mosh-style, on our cell diffs):
  1. User key event K arrives client-side.
  2. Send K upstream; locally apply guess(K) to a copy of the mirror.
  3. Render guess cells with PREDICTION attribute (dim).
  4. On FRAME_ACK from server (matched by frame_id), compare server cells
     in the affected range with our prediction.
       match → drop prediction, render normally
       mismatch → drop prediction, render authoritative
  5. On timeout (no ACK within N ms) → drop prediction.

PredictionPolicy enum: Always, Adaptive (RTT-based), Never. Configurable via TOML (phux-nz4).

Reference: Mosh SSP paper, section on prediction.
acceptance_criteria: Typing over a 200ms-RTT simulated link feels instant. Predictions render dim, reconcile within one FRAME_ACK. Snapshot test for the prediction overlay state machine. cargo nextest run -p phux-client passes; clippy -D warnings clean.
notes:
Spec refs:
  SPEC.md:150-161 — §6 FRAME_ACK (the reconciliation signal)
  ADR/0007-mosh-class-transport-and-satellites.md — load-bearing rationale

Verify:
  cargo nextest run -p phux-client predict::
  cargo clippy -p phux-client -- -D warnings
  Manual: `tc qdisc add dev lo root netem delay 200ms` then type rapidly

title: Wire codec scaffold: TLV framing per SPEC Appendix A
description: Big-endian, length-prefixed frames with custom TLV. Field IDs per Appendix A. Encoder writes into &mut Vec<u8> (or bytes::BytesMut); decoder takes &[u8] and returns Result<(Frame, &[u8] remaining), DecodeError>. Module: crates/phux-protocol/src/wire/.

phux-core domain types with SlotMap-based stable IDs (SessionId, WindowId, PaneId), a layout tree, attach/detach lifecycle. phux-server runtime exposes them over the wire format from phux-6yl via Unix domain socket. tokio current-thread runtime throughout.

title: phux-roz follow-up: tighten terminal reset on signal — held Termios + consumer-owned guard
description:
Today's phux-roz fix installs SIGINT/SIGTERM/SIGHUP handlers + a panic hook that run terminal_reset_on_signal before exit. The reset is BEST-EFFORT: it re-cooks the terminal (forces ICANON + ECHO + ISIG flags on) rather than restoring the user's original Termios, because the saved Termios lives in the RawModeGuard which the signal handler can't safely reach.

Acceptable today (the user's outer shell still works after; user can 'reset' if anything sticks), but a cleaner shape:

1. Move saved Termios into a process-global Mutex<Option<Termios>> that:
   - RawModeGuard::install_with_stdout populates on entry.
   - terminal_reset_on_signal reads on signal/panic.
   - RawModeGuard::Drop reads + clears on normal unwind.
   The signal-safety of accessing a Mutex from a tokio signal arm is fine (the arm runs on the runtime thread, not in raw signal-handler context).

2. Consider whether ALT_SCREEN_ACTIVE (currently a SeqCst AtomicBool) should also live alongside, or whether the Termios's presence is itself the signal.

3. The 'libghostty renderer still writes to real stdout because its iterators aren't generic over Write' note in the wave-5 agent's design call could be revisited if a future test or alternate consumer surface needs the renderer to write into a captured buffer.

Naturally cascades with the L1 rename and the consumer-side detach policy (phux-it8 follow-up): if 'detach' becomes a consumer choice rather than a server-driven event, the consumer's RawModeGuard cleanup becomes the only place the reset story matters.

Commit reference: 63dc6ff.

title: phux-7vx follow-up: per-Terminal TERM as L1 SPAWN field; restore TERM=ghostty when round-trip is proven
description:
Today's phux-7vx fix swaps the default TERM=ghostty -> TERM=xterm-256color on the server-spawned PTY (pane_actor.rs:default_shell_command). Reason: TERM=ghostty's terminfo advertises 'fullkbd' which newer ncurses interprets as 'kitty keyboard protocol safe', so htop/less/vim/etc. push CSI > N u, libghostty pivots to CSI-u for every keypress, and TUIs that don't speak kitty (like htop) drop quit/F-keys.

Two follow-on tasks live under this:

1. PROVE THE ROUND-TRIP. Validate phux's input/output layer round-trips kitty KIP correctly for at least: htop, less, vim, neovim, fzf, btop. Integration tests using the wave-4 Screen harness (Screen helper at crates/phux-server/tests/common/screen.rs) where possible, manual smoke tests where not. Once this is green, restore TERM=ghostty default and the user gets ghostty's full terminfo capabilities.

2. EXPOSE TERM AS L1 SPAWN CONFIG. Under ADR-0015 the L1 SPAWN message creates a Terminal. The PTY env (TERM, LANG, PHUX_*, etc.) is part of that spawn. Today the server hardcodes TERM in default_shell_command. Move to a per-Terminal env-map field in the SPAWN request so a consumer (TUI or agent) can override it. Default value lives in phux-config (when that crate gets fleshed out). Blocked by L1 cascade for the wire-message shape.

Commit reference: 9734d42 (the current hardcoded swap).

title: phux-it8 follow-up: reshape EOF as L1 TERMINAL_CLOSED + consumer-side detach policy
description:
Today's phux-it8 fix has the server send FrameKind::Detached when a pane's PTY EOFs. Under ADR-0015's L1 substrate, the more honest shape is:
- Server emits TERMINAL_CLOSED { terminal_id, reason, exit_status } (an L1 lifecycle event)
- The CONSUMER decides what to do:
  - A TUI with no remaining terminals in its attached Collection may choose to detach itself
  - An agent SDK consumer just notes the exit code and continues subscribing/observing
  - A recorder consumer logs the close and keeps going

Currently the server bakes the TUI's 'no terminals left -> detach' policy in. Behaviorally identical for v0.1 single-pane sessions; only matters once Collections support multi-Terminal lifecycles and non-TUI consumers exist.

Implementation when this gets picked up:
- Rename FrameKind::Detached's role: detach as a state change owned by the client request, separate from terminal lifecycle.
- Add FrameKind::TerminalClosed { terminal_id, reason, exit_status } per ADR-0015 §L1 lifecycle.
- Move the 'no panes -> detach client' policy out of server runtime; into TUI consumer code (likely crates/phux-client/src/attach/driver.rs main_loop).
- Update phux-server/tests/eof_detach.rs to assert TerminalClosed arrives; add a separate TUI-consumer test that asserts the TUI detaches in response.

Blocked by: L1 vocabulary cascade (PaneId -> TerminalId rename; see phux-byc.5 / ADR-0015 §Doc impact / ADR-0016).

Commit reference: b5b7617 (the current shape).

title: RenderState::update second-call diagnosis (deferred from 460faeb)
description:
Wave-2 agent's regression test (call render() twice in a row, assert Dirty::Clean on second) hit Error::InvalidValue from libghostty. Agent dropped the test rather than diagnose. Production paths (server SnapshotSynthesizer + client PaneRenderer) work fine — they each call snapshot.set_dirty(Dirty::Clean) between updates. The contract IS clear from libghostty source: Snapshot holds &mut RenderState; you can update again after Snapshot drops.

InvalidValue comes from .dirty().and_then(|v| v.try_into().map_err(|_| Error::InvalidValue)) — FFI returned a Dirty value outside {Clean, Partial, Full}. Likely the test was missing the set_dirty(Clean) reset between updates, OR libghostty returns an out-of-band Dirty value in a specific scenario the agent didn't characterize.

Tiny investigation: reconstruct the failing test scenario, find the actual cause, either fix the test or file a libghostty upstream issue if the FFI is returning an unmodeled value.

title: ADR cleanup: refresh 0004/0006/0007/0008/0010/0012 references to diff-based architecture (post-ADR-0013)
description:
ADR-0013 supersedes ADR-0002 (diff-based protocol). Six other ADRs reference the diff-based architecture and now read stale:

- ADR-0004 line 10: 'source of truth for diffs (ADR-0002)' wording — libghostty-vt is now source of truth for bytes forwarded and snapshots synthesized, not diffs.
- ADR-0006 line 179: brief reference, low impact.
- ADR-0007 line 36: Mosh-decomposition table row 'Cell-level state + diffs | Already done. This is the bet of ADR-0002. | phux-protocol::diff' — wrong. Update the bet to ADR-0013 and the location to 'phux-protocol::wire::frame::PaneOutput'. Recommend an ADR-0008-style 'What this ADR replaces' amendment pattern.
- ADR-0008 §1: 'what stays phux-defined' lists Cell, Grid, DiffOp, CursorState, CursorShape — most are going away. Update to reflect that wire-side definitions shrink to envelopes + PANE_OUTPUT + PANE_SNAPSHOT bytes payload.
- ADR-0010 lines 49-58, 248-249: native>CC argument leans on cell-diffs. Still wins under ADR-0013 (automatic libghostty feature parity + simpler wire + bytes-friendliness for non-libghostty consumers), but prose needs a refresh.
- ADR-0012 line 202: layout snapshots flow through diff frames — minor; the snapshot mechanism changes but layout-on-wire is still structured (lifecycle concern, not pane content).

Single docs commit. No code touched. Hashimoto-bar prose preserved.

title: client: configurable detach key binding
description: The phux-9gw.3 attach loop hardcodes Ctrl-b d as the detach chord. Lift this into phux-config so users can rebind. Likely lands in tandem with the broader keybinding-binding surface defined in DESIGN.md.

title: Define MouseProtocol and MouseEncoding enums per SPEC §8.5 mode bits
description: SPEC §8.5 specifies PaneModes carries a 4-bit MOUSE_PROTOCOL field (0x00F0) and a 4-bit MOUSE_ENCODING field (0x0F00), but does not enumerate the values. phux-429 landed PaneModes with u8 accessors and a TODO; this ticket defines the enums once input plumbing demands them. Likely values: MouseProtocol = { Off, X10, Normal, ButtonEvent, AnyEvent }; MouseEncoding = { Default, Utf8, Sgr, Urxvt } per common terminal practice. Coordinate with libghostty-vt's mouse:: types — if it has equivalent enums we should re-export per ADR-0008.

title: phux-server::downsample: switch to Cow if benchmarks justify
description:
Follow-up to phux-2li. The downsample helper API in `phux-server::downsample`
takes `&DiffOp` and returns an owned `DiffOp`. The TrueColor fast path
therefore unnecessarily clones. Caller is currently expected to skip the fn
when the cap is TrueColor; this leaves the optimization at the call site,
not in the helper.

If profiling on the fanout layer ever shows clone overhead is measurable,
switch the signature to `Cow<'a, DiffOp>`. API is stable today specifically
because byc.5 hasn't built fanout yet.

title: Perceptual color distance (CIELAB) for downsample — better Indexed16 picks
description:
Follow-up to phux-2li. The downsample helper currently uses naive squared
Euclidean distance in RGB space for nearest-palette lookup. This is the
standard "good enough" baseline but well-known to mispick on the 16-color
tier in particular (e.g., dark blue often loses to magenta because R is
weighted equally with G).

Migrate to a perceptual metric:

- Convert RGB → CIELAB (sRGB gamma decode + linear transform).
- Distance = Euclidean in Lab (or CIEDE2000 if we want to be fancy).

Out of scope for the substrate ticket because (a) it would expand the test
matrix significantly, and (b) the choice is reversible — the API
(`downsample(Color, ColorSupport)`) is stable; only the internals change.

title: Stress-test 'frontend-agnostic' claim (ADR-0010): non-TUI client or soften ADR
description:
## Goal

Validate that phux's wire protocol is **genuinely** frontend-agnostic, not nominally so. ADR-0010 makes "frontend-agnostic" a load-bearing identity claim, but the reference implementation has only ever served a TUI client. No non-TUI consumer has exercised the wire format, so subtle TUI-coupling could exist in the protocol that nobody will notice until someone tries to build a non-TUI client.

This ticket forces the question before v0.1 tags.

## Two paths (pick one)

1. **Build a minimal non-TUI reference client.** Stress-test what breaks; fix gaps in the protocol. Candidates, cheapest first:
   - **Structured-output exporter** — a CLI that subscribes to PANE_SNAPSHOT + diffs and emits JSON/JSONL of cell state. Tests whether the protocol carries enough information to reconstruct a pane without VT semantics.
   - **Recording/replay viewer** — captures wire traffic and replays it offline. Tests `FrameId` ordering and `FRAME_ACK` semantics in isolation from a live PTY.
   - **egui/wgpu GUI client** — most expensive, most thorough. Probably overkill for validation alone.

2. **Soften ADR-0010's central decision to "TUI-first, non-TUI not precluded."** Honest if (1) is too expensive. Keeps the must-not-preclude invariants; drops the implication that we've validated them.

## Decision criteria

Pick (2) unless someone is going to actually build the client. (1) without follow-through is theater. P3 because this is a pre-v0.1 concern, not a current blocker — but it MUST be resolved before v0.1 tags or the ADR is misleading.

## Risk list to investigate during (1) if pursued

- **`RenderingMode::VtReplay` path (SPEC §6.2).** SPEC splits `Diff` and `VtReplay` modes; the VtReplay path is probably under-specified for non-VT renderers. What does a non-VT consumer do with VtReplay frames — refuse the session? Translate via libghostty-vt in the client? Spec needs an answer.
- **`PaneDiff::ops` assume 2D grid consumers.** `PUT_CELL`/`RUN`/`COPY_RECT`/`CLEAR_RECT` are grid ops. A structured-output / accessibility viewer would need a different abstraction layer. Is the right answer "consumers project ops onto whatever model they want," or does the protocol need a higher-level abstraction?
- **Input atoms re-exported from libghostty (ADR-0006/0008) are TUI-shaped at their core.** KEY/MOUSE/FOCUS/PASTE atoms reflect terminal-input semantics. A GUI consumer would translate at the boundary — that's fine, but is the translation lossy or lossless? Worth proving with a working translator.
- **Status-bar widgets (DESIGN.md) emit ANSI sequences for clients to render.** A GUI client wants structured data — does a `StatusFrame` wire type need to exist? Currently the answer is "TUI-only widget output," which is exactly the kind of TUI-coupling ADR-0010 forbids in principle but permits in practice.

## Cross-refs

- ADR-0010 (this ticket exists because of its validation-status section)
- ADR-0006 / ADR-0008 (libghostty atom re-export — the load-bearing TUI dependency)
- SPEC §6.2 (`RenderingMode`, `ServerFeature`)
- SPEC §8 (cell-diff protocol — the surface a non-TUI client must consume)
- DESIGN §14 (out-of-scope GUI client mention)

title: Migrate Cell::text from Vec<char> to SmallVec for inline storage
description:
**Problem**

`Cell::text` is currently `Vec<char>` per cell (`crates/phux-protocol/src/diff/cell.rs:32`). For an 80x24 grid that's 1920 cells x 24-byte Vec headers = ~46KB pointer overhead alone, plus a heap allocation per non-empty cell. At 200x60 it's ~12000 headers. The common case (ASCII, single-grapheme) doesn't need a heap allocation.

**Fix**

Swap to `SmallVec<[char; 2]>`: one base codepoint + one common combining char inline. Empty cells and ASCII use zero heap.

**Why now**

Cheap pre-renderer. Once 9gw.3 lands and the renderer assumes `Vec<char>`, this becomes intrusive.

**Wire format**

Unaffected. The hand-written wire codec encodes length-prefix + chars; the in-memory collection type is invisible to the wire. Verified via diff_spike byte-identical output.
close_reason: Landed in 5c5c1d6. Cell::text now stack-allocates for grapheme clusters of length <= 2 (covers empty cells, ASCII, and base+single-combining — the common case). Wire format unchanged; verified via diff_spike byte-identical output (md5 004fe6c4a8f2ad4e61ee8054fb459fbd). smallvec gated behind the protocol's `server` feature, so the publish-clean default build is unaffected.

title: Status bar composition + client render integration

  pub struct StatusBar {

  }

        // Pad/truncate to width, group by alignment.

title: Publish phux-protocol v0.0.1 placeholder to crates.io for docs.rs
description:
Get docs.rs builds working for phux-protocol so external users can read the wire-format docs. Requires removing publish=false from phux-protocol, bumping version, and either gating or vendoring the libghostty-vt git dep (crates.io disallows git deps in published crates).

## Required Skills
rust,cargo-publish,docs.rs
design:
Sub-steps:

1. Decide libghostty-vt handling:
   - Option A: Make libghostty-vt an OPTIONAL dep behind a 'server' feature.
     phux-protocol public API uses re-exported types only when 'server' is
     enabled. Default features OMIT libghostty-vt. docs.rs builds default
     features → no git dep → publishable.
   - Option B: Wait for libghostty-vt to land on crates.io upstream.
   - Option C: Carve a no-libghostty subset of phux-protocol (likely too
     invasive given ADR-0008's 'use libghostty types directly').
   Recommend A.

2. crates/phux-protocol/Cargo.toml:
   - Remove publish = false
   - Bump version = '0.0.1' (override workspace.package.version locally,
     or bump workspace.package.version and let phux-protocol inherit while
     other private crates keep publish = false).
   - Add [features] section: server = ['libghostty-vt']
   - Move libghostty-vt from dependencies to optional dep with no default
     feature, or under [target.'cfg(...)'.dependencies].

3. Cargo.toml workspace: add docs.rs metadata to phux-protocol:
   [package.metadata.docs.rs]
   all-features = true

4. Local verify:
   cargo publish -p phux-protocol --dry-run
   cargo doc --no-deps -p phux-protocol
   (with and without --features server)

5. cargo publish -p phux-protocol (live, requires CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN).

6. Confirm https://docs.rs/phux-protocol/0.0.1 renders.

Caveat: this is the first place we'd hit the consequence of phux-protocol
re-exporting libghostty types in its PUBLIC api (ADR-0008). The 'server'
feature gating is the standard escape hatch. Document this in ADR-0008.
acceptance_criteria: docs.rs/phux-protocol/0.0.1 renders the type docs. 'cargo publish -p phux-protocol --dry-run' clean. Other workspace crates stay publish=false.

title: Satellite / federation v0.2 — hub-and-spoke across hosts
description:
Multi-host phux: one phux-server acts as hub, exposes sessions from peer phux-servers (satellites) over QUIC and SSH-stdio transports. Killer use case: AI-agent sandboxes — spin up disposable VMs, attach to all of them from one local hub.

Deferred to v0.2. ADR-0007 captures the design and the v0.1 must-not-preclude invariants so this stays additive.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `phux satellite add devbox ssh://devbox` registers a satellite via SSH-stdio.
- [ ] `phux ls` from the hub shows local and satellite sessions, namespaced as `local://name` and `sat://devbox/name`.
- [ ] Attaching to a satellite session works transparently — same input, diff, FRAME_ACK semantics as a local pane.
- [ ] Satellite disconnect mid-frame: hub buffers input, pane goes amber, reattach within 30s replays via snapshot fallback.
- [ ] QUIC transport (quinn) implemented and exercised in CI integration tests.
- [ ] Hub does NOT re-encode VT bytes; encoders stay per-satellite per ADR-0006.
- [ ] No regressions to single-host v0.1 ergonomics (Unix socket transport remains default).

## Out of scope for this epic

- Mesh topology (rejected in ADR-0007).
- Mosh wire-compatibility (rejected in ADR-0007).
- Direct client → satellite shortcuts (NAT-punching, discovery, etc.).
- Central registry / OIDC auth.

title: Config (TOML) + widget-first status bar
description:
TOML config loader with schema (per DESIGN.md), keybind resolver, status-bar architected as a widget/plugin system from day one (default config is just one composition of widgets). Validates the 'agents read/write the config' premise.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `~/.config/phux/phux.toml` loads at startup. Missing file → sensible defaults. Malformed file → clear error pointing to line:col.
- [ ] An agent can programmatically generate a valid phux.toml from a JSON spec (round-trip test in tests/).
- [ ] Status bar is composed from a `[[status.widgets]]` list. ≥2 widgets ship in-tree (`time`, `session-name`) as examples of the WidgetSpec contract.
- [ ] Keybinds resolve correctly under modifier combinations matching libghostty key-encode (snapshot test).
- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p phux-config` green; clippy -D warnings clean.

value: ADR-0013 (landed 2026-05-25, commit ac146e2) SUPERSEDES ADR-0002. Wire is now ASYMMETRIC: server→client carries VT bytes (PANE_OUTPUT {pane_id, seq, bytes} at type 0x90; PANE_SNAPSHOT {pane_id, cols, rows, vt_replay_bytes, scrollback_bytes?} at 0x91), client→server stays structured (KeyEvent/MouseEvent/etc. as libghostty atoms). Libghostty Terminal runs on BOTH ends. The diff-based architecture (DiffOp/Cell/CursorState-on-wire/DiffMirror) is GONE — deleted in 460faeb (−2443 LOC). Do not propose re-adding structured cell diffs; the cost-model error that motivated them is documented in ADR-0013's Rationale. RenderState dirty tracking is per-end render-perf, NOT wire format. See SPEC §8 (rewritten in 2071f8f) for normative shape.

ADR-0006 ('input mirrors libghostty') and ADR-0008 ('use libghostty types directly') together govern crates/phux-protocol/src/input/ and crates/phux-server/src/input/. ADR-0008 SUPERSEDES the parts of ADR-0006 that claimed discriminant equality — phux now re-exports libghostty's KeyAction/PhysicalKey/ModSet/MouseAction/MouseButton/FocusEvent directly (they ARE libghostty's types). KeyEvent/MouseEvent are still phux wire-friendly structs because libghostty's Event<'alloc> is allocator-lifetime-bound. The encoder still lives server-side; never on the wire. Read both ADRs before touching input code.

Libghostty is the right foundation for phux's VT layer — confirmed by user 2026-05-25 after comparing to rmux, which rolled its own custom tmux-compatible VT state machine. Why: libghostty gives parity with Ghostty (Kitty graphics protocol, modern key protocol, sixel) that a hand-rolled parser would have to re-implement piecemeal forever. How to apply: do not propose replacing libghostty-rs with a custom parser; do not treat 'we could write our own' as a serious alternative in ADRs; when evaluating libghostty pin bumps or API changes, the answer is to adapt phux, not fork off libghostty. See ADR-0006 and ADR-0008. Related: [[rmux-comparative]], [[adr-input-rules]], [[libghostty-rs-pin]].

libghostty-rs is pinned to a git rev in Cargo.toml (not crates.io 0.1.1, which is missing link-static and kitty-graphics features). Do NOT bump to a published version — read the pinned rev from Cargo.lock and stay there unless explicitly told to upgrade. Source dir for reading libghostty-vt internals: target/debug/build/libghostty-vt-*/out or the cargo-resolved git checkout under ~/.cargo/git/checkouts/libghostty-rs-*/.

libghostty Terminal/RenderState are !Send + !Sync (holds NonNull<TerminalImpl>; no unsafe impl in libghostty source). phux uses current_thread tokio runtime (ADR-0003) which makes this fine. Client driver.rs uses #[allow(clippy::future_not_send)] with explanatory comments — this is intentional, not drift. For server-side per-pane Terminal placement (coming in byc.8), tokio::spawn is wrong — needs spawn_local on a LocalSet OR an actor-pattern with mpsc channels. See phux-byc.8-placement ticket. NEVER use Arc<Mutex<Terminal>> — won't compile.

Linear git history is the default on phux. Use fast-forward merges or rebases, NOT --no-ff merge commits. For a multi-branch swarm integration: for each agent branch in dependency order, 'git rebase main <branch> && git checkout main && git merge --ff-only <branch>'. Each agent's branch should be a single squashed commit (tell them so in the prompt). The git log MUST stay linear and bisect-friendly. Wave 1 used --no-ff merges; the rule kicks in from wave 2 onward.

Mosh's value decomposes into 3 parts: (1) cell-level diffs — DONE, ADR-0002, SPEC §8; (2) predictive local echo — client-side feature, transport-agnostic, lives in phux-client and reconciles on FRAME_ACK (SPEC §6); (3) UDP State Sync Protocol — REJECTED, use QUIC instead via quinn for v0.2+. Mosh wire-compatibility (mosh-client attaching to phux) is out of scope unless iOS/embedded becomes a concrete use case. Do not reimplement SSP. See ADR-0007.

Protocol module pattern: when adding a new module to crates/phux-protocol/src/, mirror the structure of crates/phux-protocol/src/diff/ (sibling files for distinct types, mod.rs that re-exports, top-level lib.rs that re-exports the headline names). Tests live in #[cfg(test)] mod tests at the bottom of the file they test. Cross-file integration tests live in crates/<crate>/tests/ as separate test binaries.

- Working-tree desync trap: `git update-ref refs/heads/main feat-branch` advances the ref but does NOT update the checkout's working tree if HEAD was on main. After ref-update, `git reset --hard HEAD` to sync working tree. This bit the parent agent 3+ times today. SPEC.md is the single source of truth for wire format and behavior. Section anchors: §7 message catalog (line 223), §8 hot path / cells / diffs / cursor (line 407), §9 input (line 601, with subsections 9.1=key, 9.2=mouse, 9.3=focus, 9.4=paste), §10.5 viewport resize (line 1022), Appendix A encoding primitives (line 1300). Any wire-format change requires a SPEC edit in the same PR.

Transport invariant (ADR-0007): all I/O in phux-server and phux-client MUST go through an async Transport trait. v0.1 ships only UnixSocketTransport, but no domain code may name a concrete transport type. This keeps QUIC + SSH-stdio transports (for satellites) purely additive in v0.2+. Also: SessionId is a tagged union { LOCAL(u32) | SATELLITE { host, id } } on the wire from day one — v0.1 only constructs LOCAL but decoders MUST accept SATELLITE and return UnsupportedSatelliteRoute error.

phux's default is to USE libghostty's types directly via re-export, never duplicate them. Where libghostty has a plain (non-allocator-bound) type for something, that IS the phux type — pub use libghostty_vt::*. This applies to: input atoms (key::{Key, Action, Mods}, mouse::{Action, Button}, focus::Event), style atoms (style::{StyleColor, Underline, RgbColor, PaletteIndex}). Allocator-bound types (key::Event<'alloc>, mouse::Event<'alloc>) and multiplexer concepts (Cell, Grid, DiffOp, sessions/windows/panes, FrameKind) stay phux-defined. Wire byte stability is preserved separately via phux-owned tag values in wire/diff.rs — never blind 'as u32'. See ADR-0008.

Server-side wire→PaneActor dispatch was historically the weak seam: pty_pump.rs tests poke PaneActor input mpsc directly, so the handle_client fallthrough on FrameKind::InputKey/InputMouse/InputFocus (crates/phux-server/src/runtime.rs:601 'other =>' arm) went undetected through byc.4's close and byc.6.5's filing. Going forward: every new FrameKind that affects pane state needs a wire-level integration test that drives handle_client (the production read loop), not just the downstream component in isolation. The byc.6.* test plan (under crates/phux-server/tests/) is the right venue.

Worktree isolation discipline for parallel agent fan-out: ALWAYS pre-create worktrees explicitly with 'git worktree add /tmp/phux-<wave>-<task> -b <branch-name> main' BEFORE launching agents. Tell each agent in their prompt to 'cd /tmp/phux-<wave>-<task> && pwd' as the first action. Do NOT rely on the Agent tool's isolation:worktree flag for parallel launches — in wave 1 only 2 of 4 agents got real worktrees (race condition), the other 2 shared the main checkout. Self-managed worktrees are race-free.
